titleStmt.title: SII 16.255: original edition by H. K. Narasimhaswami and G. S. Gai
No. 255. (A.R. No. 530 of 1913.) VINUKOṆḌA, VINUKONDA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.

summary.p: On a stone in the verandah of the Sub-Registrare's Office.
This fragmentary inscription is dated Śaka 1484, Dundubhi, Āshāḍha śu. 11, Thursday corresponding to 1562 A.D., June 13. The weekday, however was Saturday.

It registers a gift of 3 kuñchas of land to Aṁdela Nāguṁggayyala Vīrayya and Saṅkā Vīrayya-seṭṭi by Daḷavāyi Ōbaḷēśu, the kāryakarta of Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Koṇḍrājayyadēva-mahārāju for maintaining a flower garden in the temple of Raghunāyaka at Vinukoṇḍa in Vinukoṇḍa-sīma. Vinukoṇḍa-sīma is said to have been held by Koṇḍrājayyadēva-mahārāju as nāyaṅkara from Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāmarāju Tirumalayyadēva-mahārāju.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
. svasti śrījayābhyudaya śālivā
hana śakavaruṣaṁbulu 1484 Agu
nēṭi duṁdubhi saṁvvatsara Āṣāḍha śu
ddha 11 gu . śrīmadrājādhirāja rā
japaramēśvara śrīvīrapratāpa śrīsadā
śivadēva mahārāyalu vidyānagara
maṁdu ratnaśiṁhvāsanārūdhulai pṛ
thivisāṁmrājyamu śēyucuṁḍḍaṁ
gāsu . śrīmanmahāmaṁḍḍalēśvara veṁka
ṭēśu komāra koṁḍrājeṁyya dēvama
hārājayavāri kāryakartalaina daḷuvāyi
vōbiḷēśuniṁgāru Aṁdela nāguṁggayyala
vīrayya saṁkā vīrayyaseṭṭikini yiccina dha
rmaśāsanakramameṭlannanu . śrīmanmahāma
ṁḍalēśvara rāmarāju tirumalarājayya
dēvamahārājayyavāru koṁḍrājayya
vāri nāyaṁkarānaku pāliṁcci yiyyanava
dhariṁccina vinukoṁḍa śīmalōnu vinukoṁḍḍa śrī
raghunāyakulaku tōṁṭaśēśēṁṭaṁduku mī
ku jītabhatyālaku śelavugānu tōṁṭa paḍamaṭa
nu mīku kēsaripāṭini mūḍu kucyaḷa kṣētra
mu yistimi ganaka Ā kṣētrānaṁ baṁḍḍina nānādhā
nyamunu mī putrapautrapāraṁpparyamu Ā
caṁdrārkasthāyigānu Anubhaviṁcci dēvunitōṭa
nānāphalālu nānāpuvvulu virivigānu cē
sukhāna bratukuvāru . duṁdubhi saṁvatsara
śuddha 5 lu . śrīmanmahāmaṁḍḍalēśvara veṁkaṭēśvaraya The continuation is lost.
Digital edition of SII 16.255 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
